title: AI Process Automation Engineer
company_stage: Growth stage (125-person team, established product, high-growth)
company_focus:
AI-native market research and decision intelligence platform
that automates customer insights
description:
About Fuel Cycle:

Fuel Cycle is the Autonomous Insights Platform trusted by
some of the world's most iconic brands — including Sonos,
Carhartt, Petco, Glassdoor, Thomson Reuters, and Abercrombie
& Fitch. Headquartered in Los Angeles (Wilshire Blvd) with
offices in New York, we are a 125-person team on a mission
to accelerate decision intelligence: turning customer
research into confident business action in hours, not weeks.

Our platform unifies quantitative, qualitative, and UX
research into a single AI-native system — delivering 85%
faster time-to-insight, 10x cost savings per study, and 150x
ROI versus traditional research vendors. Agentic AI runs
studies end-to-end, so insight teams spend their time on
judgment and strategy rather than production and logistics.

This role exists because we are living what we sell. Our
leadership team made a deliberate decision to go all-in on
Claude and agentic AI — not just in our product, but across
every internal function. Our Marketing team has already
deployed five production Claude Code agents (competitive
intelligence, sales call synthesis, feature launch
automation, and more), saving 10–17 hours of manual work per
week with a three-person team. Sales, Research, Community,
and Panels are next. The difference between being AI-enabled
and AI-native is the entire bet, and this hire is part of
closing that gap.

We are building a shared system of memory, skills, and
agents that the whole company runs on — not a collection of
individual setups. The person in this role will be a
foundational contributor to that architecture, working
directly alongside our Founder/Chairman, Product,
Engineering, and cross-functional operations leads to make
Fuel Cycle AI-native from the inside out.

Our values: Customer Obsessed. Team Before Self. Extreme
Impact. Endless Curiosity.

Why work at Fuel Cycle?

Join a high-growth team where curiosity is valued, ownership
is encouraged, and your work drives real-world impact.
Whether you’re based at our Los Angeles HQ, New York City
hub, working remotely across the U.S., or part of our global
team in India, you’ll help shape the future of decision
intelligence for some of the world’s most iconic brands.

Overview:

Fuel Cycle's leadership has made a clear, urgent bet: the
difference between being AI-enabled and AI-native is the
entire game. We are looking for a sharp, curious, and
technically motivated recent graduate to help us win it — as
an AI Process Automation Engineer writing Claude Code to
agentify how Fuel Cycle operates from the inside out.

This is not a greenfield exploration role. Our Marketing
team has already deployed five production Claude Code agents
— automating competitive intelligence, Gong call synthesis,
feature launch campaigns, and GTM release workflows — saving
10–17 hours of manual work per week with a three-person
team. The architecture is built to scale. Research delivery,
Community, Panels, Sales, and Finance are next. You will be
the dedicated builder expanding that system across the
organization.

You will work directly alongside our Founder/Chairman,
Product & Engineering teams and the cross-functional leads
who are driving this initiative — translating business
process challenges into robust, production-grade agentic
solutions built on a shared foundation of memory, skills,
and reusable Claude Code agents.

Location: Los Angeles

Work Arrangement: This is a fully onsite role requiring five
days per week in our Brentwood office.

Key Responsibilities:

- Expand and maintain the existing suite of Claude Code agents
  — starting with the Marketing agents already in production
  (competitive intelligence, Gong synthesis, GTM automation)
  and extending the model into Research delivery, Community
  operations, Panels, Sales, and Finance.
- Partner with department heads and operations leads across
  Fuel Cycle to map high-volume manual processes — in both
  our research and community services operation and our
  internal functions — and design agentic replacements.
- Build and test multi-step agentic pipelines that integrate
  Claude with Fuel Cycle's existing tool stack, including
  Asana, Gong, and third-party APIs, using the shared
  knowledge base architecture already in place.
- Contribute to the shared system of memory, skills, and
  agents the whole team runs on — ensuring every agent reads
  from consistent brand voice, ICP profiles, and process
  documentation so outputs are accurate and on-standard
  without manual prompt engineering per task.
- Monitor deployed automations for accuracy, performance, and
  edge-case failures; produce a playbook for each agent
  shipped so other teams can understand, replicate, and
  build on the model.
- Stay current on the Claude API, Claude Code releases, and
  agentic design patterns — proactively surfacing
  improvements and bringing new capabilities to the
  attention of product and engineering leads.
- Work with the AI transformation leads to push automation
  knowledge out through the organization and help teams move
  fast on their own functions.

Your Success Metrics:

- Automation Impact: Measurable reduction in manual work hours
  across deployed agents, benchmarked against pre-automation
  baselines.
- Agent Velocity: Consistent delivery of production-grade
  agents on a quarterly cadence, spanning Sales, Research,
  Community, Panels, and Finance within the first year.
- Reliability & Documentation: All deployed agents maintain
  low error/failure rates in production and are accompanied
  by a reusable playbook enabling other teams to build on
  the work.
- Architectural Contribution: Active contribution to the
  shared memory, skills, and agent library — measured by
  reuse of components across agents and reduction in per-
  task prompt engineering overhead.

Who you’ll work with?

- Founder
- Engineering
- Product
- Department Heads (Marketing, Sales, Research Excellence,
  Audience Experience, Audience Acquisition, Finance, etc.)

Core Skills, Competencies & Attributes:

Technical Skills

- Proficiency in Python; comfort writing scripts, working with
  JSON/APIs, and debugging logic errors.
- Familiarity with LLMs and an ability to craft clear,
  effective prompts — coursework, side projects, or personal
  experimentation counts.
- Working knowledge of REST APIs and experience integrating
  third-party services (Zapier, Make, or custom API calls).
- Basic Git fluency (commit, branch, pull request workflow).
- Exposure to at least one cloud or SaaS environment (AWS,
  GCP, Notion, Salesforce, HubSpot, etc.).

Attributes & Mindset

- Strong problem-solving instinct — you break big ambiguous
  challenges into small, testable steps.
- Clear written and verbal communication; able to explain
  technical decisions to non-technical stakeholders.
- Self-starter who takes ownership of projects end-to-end
  without needing constant direction.
- Genuine enthusiasm for AI tools and a curiosity about how
  they can reshape how companies operate.

What you’ll bring:

Education

- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems,
  Engineering, or a related technical field (May 2023–May
  2025 graduates are welcome to apply).

Preferred, but Not Required

- Prior experience using the Anthropic Claude API or Claude
  Code (even on personal projects).
- Familiarity with agentic frameworks such as LangChain,
  CrewAI, or similar.
- Internship or project experience in a SaaS, startup, or
  technology operations environment.
- Exposure to workflow automation tools (Zapier,
  Make/Integromat, n8n).
- Experience with no-code/low-code tools that bridge technical
  and business teams.

Benefits & Perks:

Fuel Cycle is committed to supporting the well-being,
flexibility, and growth of our team. We offer a competitive
and inclusive benefits package that includes:

- Comprehensive Health Coverage: Medical, dental, and vision
  insurance plans
- 401(k) with Company Match: Plan for your future with our
  retirement savings program
- Equity Purchase Option: Participate in Fuel Cycle’s long-
  term success
- Flexible Work Schedule: Empowering you to balance life and
  work
- Generous Time Off:
  - 15 vacation days and 7 sick days per year
  - 12 company holidays
  - 4 floating holidays/recharge days to rest or celebrate what
    matters to you
- Paid Parental Leave: Time to bond with your growing family
- Monthly Internet & Phone Stipend: Support for remote work
  setup
- Wellness & Lifestyle Perks: Access to tools like Rightway
  (healthcare navigation), Headspace (mental wellness),
  Peloton (fitness), and more.
- Team Connection Perks:
  - Weekly community lunches, refreshments, and snacks at our LA
    & NY headquarters
  - Pet-friendly office environments

Compensation Overview:

The expected starting salary range for this position is
$89,000 - $119,000. This range represents the typical
starting compensation offered to candidates hired into this
role. Final base salary will be determined based on a
variety of factors, including location, work experience,
skills, knowledge, education, and certifications.

In addition to base salary, this role may also be eligible
for an equity grant or purchase option. These components
make up your total compensation package, which will be
reviewed in greater detail during your initial recruiter
conversation.

Commitment to Diversity, Equity, and Equal Opportunity:

At Fuel Cycle, we embrace the values of diversity, equity,
and inclusion and are committed to fostering an inclusive
company culture. We believe that everyone, regardless of
their background or identity, should have equal access to
opportunities for growth and advancement. Our selection
processes and career pathways are designed to be fair,
transparent, and free from bias. We value the unique
perspectives and contributions of each team member, knowing
that this diverse range of experiences strengthens our team.
Fuel Cycle stands firmly against discrimination based on
disability, sex, race, gender identity, sexual orientation,
religion, national origin, age, veteran status, or any other
protected status under the law.

Fuel Cycle is an equal opportunity employer and fully comply
with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA). We will
provide reasonable accommodations for qualified applicants
and employees with disabilities, as needed, to enable them
to perform the essential functions of their job and
participate in the application and interview process. If you
require accommodations during any part of the application
process, please contact us at hr@fuelcycle.com to discuss
your needs.
responsibilities: Expand and maintain production Claude Code agents across Marketing, Research delivery, Community, Panels, Sales, and Finance
Partner with department heads to map high-volume manual processes and design agentic replacements
Build and test multi-step agentic pipelines integrating Claude with tools like Asana, Gong, and third-party APIs
Contribute to shared system of memory, skills, and agents ensuring consistent brand voice and process documentation
Monitor deployed automations for accuracy, performance, and edge-case failures; create playbooks for each agent
Stay current on Claude API and agentic design patterns; surface improvements to product and engineering leads
Push automation knowledge through the organization and help teams build their own automations
use_cases: Sales call synthesis from Gong recordings
Feature launch and GTM campaign automation
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role involves directly building, deploying, and
maintaining Claude Code agents and agentic pipelines using
Claude API. The work is hands-on with LLMs - creating
production-grade agentic solutions, designing multi-step
agentic pipelines, and working with prompt engineering and
agent architecture. This is core AI/LLM development work,
not support work.
